heading: Definitions
label: 1471 Definitions
notes: References in Text, referred to in par. (1)(B)(i), (iii), has been amended, and subsecs. (b) and (c) of section 5304 no longer define the terms “Indian tribe” and “tribal organization”. However, such terms are defined elsewhere in that section.Section 5304 of title 25Act of , referred to in par. (1)(B)(ii), is , , popularly known as the Indian Reorganization Act, which was classified generally to subchapter V (§ 461 et seq.) of chapter 14 of Title 25, Indians, prior to editorial reclassification as chapter 45 (§ 5101 et seq.) of Title 25. (2). substituted “llamas, alpacas, live fish, crawfish, and other animals that” for “fish used for food, and other animals designated by the Secretary (at the Secretary’s sole discretion) that” in introductory provisions.Pub. children.label: i
children.text: an established producer or husbander of livestock or a dairy producer who is a citizen of, or legal resident alien in, the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a farm cooperative, private domestic corporation, partnership, or joint operation in which a majority interest is held by members, stockholders, or partners who are citizens of, or legal resident aliens in, the United States, if such cooperative, corporation, partnership, or joint operation is engaged in livestock production or husbandry, or dairy production; or

children.label: i
children.text: any Indian tribe (as defined in );section 5304(b) of title 252See References in Text note below.2

children.label: ii
children.text: any Indian organization or entity chartered under the Act of (, chapter 576; et seq.), commonly known as the “Indian Reorganization Act”;June 18, 193448 Stat. 98425 U.S.C. 4612

children.label: iii
children.text: any tribal organization (as defined in ); orsection 5304(c) of title 252

children.label: iv
children.text: any economic enterprise (as defined in );section 1452(e) of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: are part of a foundation herd (including producing dairy cattle) or offspring; or

children.label: B
children.text: are purchased as part of a normal operation and not to obtain additional benefits under this subchapter.
The term “State” means any State of the United States, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, or Guam.

children.label: A
children.text: best suits the livestock producer’s operation; and

children.label: B
children.text: is consistent with acceptable feed practices.
The term “area” includes any Indian reservation (as defined in section 1985(e)(1)(D)(ii) of this title).2
